description_long: This is a rustic log cabin constructed from horizontally stacked, rounded wooden logs with visible interlocking corners. The roof is a simple gable style, covered with what appears to be thatch or textured wooden shingles, giving it a natural, weathered look. The front facade features a single wooden door, slightly ajar, and a small rectangular window with a wooden frame. The rear side of the cabin has a small, single window, while the sides are solid log walls without openings. The structure sits low to the ground, with a small wooden step leading up to the entrance. The overall appearance suggests a traditional, hand-built dwelling suited for rural or forested environments.
description: A rustic log cabin with a gable thatched roof, wooden door, and small windows, built from stacked logs. The structure exudes a traditional, weathered charm.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance door, a small window to the left, and the textured gable roof.
description_view_1: Side gable view displaying the solid log wall with no windows or doors, emphasizing the roof's ridge and log construction.
description_view_2: Rear view featuring a single small window on the left side and uninterrupted log walls.
description_view_3: Opposite gable end with a solid log wall and no openings, highlighting the symmetry of the roof.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with a thatched or textured shingle covering, moderate overhangs, and no visible dormers or additional features
attribute_window_arrangement: Two small rectangular windows, one on the front facade to the left of the door and one on the rear wall, both single and spaced apart, creating an asymmetrical arrangement
attribute_door_placement: One exterior door located on the front facade, offset to the right side, adjacent to the front window
